batch_summary: In their apartment, a depressed Chandler laments his fear of dying alone, worrying he threw away his chance at love over a petty pronunciation pet peeve. Joey tries to comfort him but offers little reassurance about future holiday plans, prompting Chandler to take action. After Chandler leaves, Joey humorously plays with ceramic figurines. Determined to change his fate, Chandler makes a phone call to his ex-girlfriend Janice, who reacts with her signature shock.
cumulative_summary: In their apartment, Chandler spirals into a dramatic existential crisis, expressing deep fears of ending up alone while his friends move on to marriage and family life. He even worries that he previously sabotaged his own happiness by dumping a woman over her pronunciation of the word "supposedly." Though Joey tries to cheer him up by offering to buy him breakfast, he inadvertently worsens Chandler's anxiety by failing to promise him holiday invitations in the future, offering only a yearly Super Bowl gathering instead. Spurred by the bleak prospect of his future, Chandler resolves not to let this happen and abruptly leaves. While Joey stays behind, humorously mocking the word "supposedly" with ceramic figurines, Chandler heads to the kitchen to take matters into his own hands. He dials a number and makes a call, which is answered by his ex-girlfriend Janice, who responds with her iconic, shocked exclamation of "Oh, my God!"

batch_summary: At the coffee shop, Chandler defends calling Janice to his skeptical friends, declaring her his last chance at love. However, when Janice arrives, Chandler is shocked to discover she is heavily pregnant and married. Janice playfully admits she wanted to see his face in person rather than telling him over the phone, leaving Chandler defeated. Meanwhile, back at the apartment, Monica tries to get Rachel to play 'hide the lamp' to get rid of an ugly seashell lamp, but Rachel firmly tells her to let it go.

batch_summary: After Monica and Rachel exchange quick barbs over the seashell lamp, Ross arrives with a briefcase of fossils to prove evolution to Phoebe. Phoebe challenges his scientific certainty, arguing that history has proven "bright minds" wrong before. She pressures him to admit there is a tiny chance he could be wrong. When Ross finally concedes to a "teeny tiny possibility," Phoebe hilariously turns on him, expressing disappointment that he abandoned his belief system so easily, leaving Ross completely stunned.

batch_summary: After Ross leaves, Monica accidentally shatters Rachel's hated seashell lamp, sparking immediate accusations of sabotage. Meanwhile, Chandler's existential crisis deepens. Reeling from his encounter with Janice, he dramatically declares he is destined to die alone and hilariously plans a future as a 'crazy snake man' to cope with his isolation, prompting Monica to step in and reassure him.

batch_summary: Chandler admits to self-sabotaging his relationships, but the girls comfort him, pointing out that his willingness to reach out to Janice proves he is ready for commitment. Rachel accepts the peace offering, easing the tension between the roommates.

batch_summary: Chandler proudly announces he has a date with a coworker, Allison, proving he is overcoming his superficial relationship hang-ups. As everyone heads inside, Chandler stays behind in the hallway. After a broom falls from the empty apartment, Chandler shares a quiet, poignant moment of closure, saying a final, respectful goodbye to Mr. Heckles.

Despite his earlier resolution to overlook minor physical flaws, he immediately becomes hilariously fixated on the unusually large size of her head.
